lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Visalia Municipal Airport: SeaPort operated 12 nonstop round trips/week to Burbank and Sacramento, suspended Jan 15 2016
Wikipedia Visalia Municipal Airport: SeaPort Burbank and Sacramento service ended Jan 15 2016
Wikipedia Visalia Municipal Airport: United mainline flights began 1946 and ended November 1979

note: United served VIS 1946-1979; subsequent EAS carriers (Great Lakes, SkyWest, SeaPort) served LAX/BUR/SMF; airport lost all scheduled service after SeaPort suspension Jan 2016

lostDestinations.source: Oxnard Airport history: WestAir operated EMB-120 Brasilia as United Express nonstop to SFO in the 1990s
Oxnard Airport history: Golden West operated Twin Otter/Short 330 nonstop to SAN in 1981
Oxnard Airport history: Apollo Airways operated Jetstreams to SBA with connecting flights to SFO/SJC in 1981

note: Airport lost all scheduled service June 8 2010 when SkyWest/United Express ended LAX flights; Southwest Airways DC-3s began service 1946

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Eastern Oregon Regional Airport: United 737/727 served PDT-PDX-EUG-MFR-SFO routing until May 22 1981
Wikipedia Eastern Oregon Regional Airport: United 727s flew PDT-SLC-DEN-PHL routing until May 22 1981
Wikipedia Eastern Oregon Regional Airport: United 727s served PDT to ORD via SLC in late 1960s-1981
Wikipedia Eastern Oregon Regional Airport: Horizon served PDT to SEA via Walla Walla with Dash-8-Q400, ended late 2008
Wikipedia Eastern Oregon Regional Airport: United 737-200s flew PDT-BOI-SLC routing in late 1960s-1970s

note: United mainline jet service ended May 22 1981; Horizon/Alaska served PDT 1982-2008; Boutique Air currently provides EAS service to PDX only

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Modesto City-County Airport: SkyWest ended SFO flights June 4 2014
Wikipedia Modesto City-County Airport: SkyWest LAX service ended June 2008

detail: United served Modesto from 1946 until 1979-80; SkyWest/United Express resumed service mid-2006 with flights to LAX and SFO, ending all service on June 4 2014 when SFO flights ceased.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Salinas Municipal Airport: Air Trails served SNS to SFO and OAK until 1981
Wikipedia Salinas Municipal Airport: Air Trails served SNS to OAK until 1981
Wikipedia Salinas Municipal Airport: United offered once-daily stops northbound/southbound SFO-LAX until 1962

detail: Salinas had commercial service as early as 1933 via Pacific Seaboard Air Lines; United ended Convair service in 1962; commuter airline Air Trails was last carrier, ending service to SFO and OAK in 1981.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Chico Municipal Airport / The Orion: SkyWest ended service to San Francisco at end of 2014

detail: SkyWest Airlines operating as United Express ended the last scheduled service to San Francisco in late 2014, leaving Chico with no commercial air service; city and airport commission have been working to restore flights since.
source: Wikipedia Chico Municipal Airport; The Orion (Chico State student paper) 2019

lostDestinations.source: AirlineGeeks: SkyWest operated United Express EMB-120 service from Palomar to LAX ending April 6 2015
AirlineGeeks: Cal Jet operated CRJ700 Palomar-LAS service ending April 18 2018
AirlineGeeks: California Pacific Airlines began SJC/Reno/LAS/PHX flights Nov 2018, ceased abruptly ~Dec 2018

detail: SkyWest ended 17 years of United Express LAX service on April 6 2015; two short-lived carriers followed in 2017-2018, the last (California Pacific Airlines) ceasing all flights ~December 2018, leaving CLD without scheduled service.
source: AirlineGeeks.com 2020; San Diego County Airport press release

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Merced Regional Airport: Great Lakes operated MCE-ONT (Ontario/LA) EAS service ending 2015
Wikipedia Merced Regional Airport: United Airlines served MCE from 1946 until deregulation 1978

detail: United served Merced 1946-1978; airport has been EAS-dependent since 1982; Great Lakes Airlines, Scenic Airlines, Boutique Air, and Advanced Air have provided subsidized service; Contour Airlines selected as successor EAS carrier for 2026 with ERJ-135/145 service to LAX and Las Vegas.
source: Wikipedia Merced Regional Airport; World Airline News Oct 2021

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Imperial County Airport: SkyWest United Express EMB-120 service to LAX ended 2013
Wikipedia Imperial County Airport: United Express flew IPL-YUM-LAX routing in late 1990s

detail: Hughes Airwest ended IPL service in 1979; SkyWest/United Express provided the sole service 1997-2013; now EAS-dependent with Southern Airways Express (9-seat Cessna Grand Caravans) flying to LAX and PHX; as of Feb 2026 county evaluating competing EAS bids.
source: Wikipedia Imperial County Airport; Calexico Chronicle Feb 2026

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Inyokern Airport: Hughes Airwest F-27 service to LAX via Lancaster ended by ~1980; commuter service ended by early 1980s
Wikipedia Inyokern Airport: Southwest Airways DC-3 service to Burbank began mid-1950s under Navy contract; Pacific Air Lines flew DC-3s to LAX by 1960

detail: Inyokern (Kern County) had scheduled service from mid-1950s with Southwest Airways DC-3s to Burbank under Navy contract; Hughes Airwest F-27 flights to LAX via Lancaster ended by ~1980; all scheduled service ceased by early 1980s with no service since.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Crater Lake-Klamath Regional Airport: PenAir Saab 340B service PDX ended Aug 2017 after PenAir bankruptcy
Wikipedia Crater Lake-Klamath Regional Airport: SkyWest United Express terminated LMT-PDX and LMT-SFO service June 5 2014
Wikipedia Crater Lake-Klamath Regional Airport: Horizon Air DHC-8/Metroliner service to SEA and PDX before late 1990s exit

detail: SkyWest/United Express ended service to PDX and SFO on June 5 2014; PenAir briefly restored Saab 340B service to PDX starting Oct 2016 before ceasing service Aug 7 2017 following bankruptcy; airport has had no commercial service since.
source: Wikipedia Crater Lake-Klamath Regional Airport; FlyerTalk thread

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Astoria Regional Airport: SeaPort Airlines flew AST-PDX from March 2008 until Spring 2010
Wikipedia Astoria Regional Airport: West Coast Airlines and successors served AST until 1974-75

detail: West Coast Airlines and successor Hughes Airwest served Astoria until 1974-75; SeaPort Airlines briefly restored PDX service March 2008-Spring 2010; no commercial airline service since; Coast Guard Air Station Astoria still operates at the field.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Corvallis Municipal Airport: Hughes Airwest pulled F-27s out of CVO in 1973
Wikipedia Corvallis Municipal Airport: West Coast DC-3 service began 1947; Hughes Airwest successor ended F-27 service 1973

detail: West Coast Airlines DC-3 flights began in 1947; successor Hughes Airwest ended F-27 service to Corvallis in 1973; no scheduled airline service for over 50 years; currently general aviation only despite being home to Oregon State University.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Olympia Regional Airport: Big Sky Airlines operated OLM-GEG in early 2000s, only scheduled destination
Wikipedia Olympia Regional Airport: Horizon Air served OLM before Big Sky era

detail: Olympia is the state capital of Washington yet has no scheduled air service; Big Sky Airlines was the last carrier, flying to Spokane in the early 2000s before ceasing service; the airport is a general aviation facility 16 miles south of downtown.
source: Wikipedia Olympia Regional Airport; Simple Flying

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Grant County International Airport: Big Sky Airlines EAS service to PDX and BOI discontinued Sept 1 2006
Wikipedia Grant County International Airport: Big Sky Airlines EAS service to Boise discontinued Sept 1 2006
Wikipedia Grant County International Airport: SkyWest United Express nonstop to SEA June 2009-June 2010

detail: Big Sky Airlines provided EAS-subsidized service to Portland and Boise until September 1 2006; SkyWest/United Express briefly added SEA service June 2009-June 2010; no commercial passenger service since 2010.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Del Norte County Airport: SkyWest ended all United Express service to CEC mid-April 2015 due to FAA Part 121 rules forcing EMB-120 retirement
Wikipedia Del Norte County Airport: SkyWest served CEC-PDX until mid-April 2015
Wikipedia Del Norte County Airport: Hughes Airwest served CEC with F-27 turboprops to RDD and PDX through 1970

detail: SkyWest/United Express ended service to SFO and PDX in mid-April 2015 when FAA Part 121 rule changes forced retirement of the EMB-120 fleet; Advanced Air now provides EAS Dornier 328 Jet service to Oakland (OAK).

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia Southwest Oregon Regional Airport: United Express ended OTH-PDX nonstop Feb 16 2012
Wikipedia Southwest Oregon Regional Airport: Hughes Airwest pulled out of OTH 1979; United Express added SFO service circa 2008

detail: Hughes Airwest ended OTH service in 1979; United Express/SkyWest added SFO nonstops ~2008 and dropped PDX service Feb 2012; SeaPort Airlines and PenAir provided brief EAS PDX service 2012-2017; currently United Express (SkyWest) serves SFO only.
